solution.p: To get rid of all fractions in this equation, we multiple all terms with the common denominator. In this equation, the common denominator is {t}.

p.m: \displaystyle{\begin{aligned} {\frac{1}{t}} \amp = {-5+\frac{21}{t}} \\ \frac{1}{t} \cdot t \amp = -5 \cdot t +\frac{21}{t} \cdot t \\ 1 \amp = -5 t + 21 \\ 1 \mathbf{ - 21} \amp = -5 t + 21 \mathbf{ - 21} \\ -20 \amp = -5 t \\ \frac{-20}{-5} \amp = \frac{-5 t}{-5} \\ 4 \amp = t \\ t \amp = 4 \end{aligned} }

solution.p: We must check solutions when we solve rational equations. By the original equation, the domain condition is t \ne 0. This doesnt conflict with the solution above, so the solution is t = 4.
